FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD study data from 5.0 release with nearly 10,000 children aged 9-10 years

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time is generic composite including television, videos, video games, texting, and video chat combined (Methods: 'total digital devices'). No smartphone/SM-specific breakdown extractable. Falls under 'generic total screen time' composite criterion.

FT_IC3.evidence: Criterion contingent on FT_IC2 MET; FT_IC2 fails

FT_IC4.evidence: Criterion contingent on FT_IC2 MET; FT_IC2 fails

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using linear mixed-effects models and mediation analysis with ABCD data

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article in Acta Psychologica, 2026

FT_EC7_reason: FT_IC2 NOT_MET; exclusion criterion does not apply
notes: Paper fails at FT_IC2 (screen time composite without phone/SM breakdown). Generic screen time measure does not qualify per prompt criterion.

FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD study 5.1 data release, two-year follow-up cohort, N=1,036 participants aged ~12 years

FT_IC2.evidence: Problematic gaming severity measured via VGAQ scale. Video games platform unspecified (applies E17). Per prompt: 'Video games unspecified platform → NOT_MET (ambiguous)'. No smartphone/SM exposure specified.

FT_IC3.evidence: Criterion contingent on FT_IC2 MET; FT_IC2 fails

FT_IC4.evidence: Criterion contingent on FT_IC2 MET; FT_IC2 fails

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using connectome-based predictive modeling (CPM) with fMRI data

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article in Journal of Behavioral Addictions, 2026

FT_EC7_reason: FT_IC2 NOT_MET; exclusion criterion does not apply
notes: Paper fails at FT_IC2 (video games without specified platform; no smartphone/SM variable). Does not meet exposure criterion.

FT_IC1.evidence: Uses ABCD study longitudinal data, N=1,186 participants (551/186 cyberbullying victims/non-victims), 9-10 years at baseline

FT_IC2.evidence: Measures cyberbullying victimization alone (whether adolescents were bullied via social media in past 12 months). No separately-measured smartphone/SM variable. Applies E6: 'Cyberbullying alone without separately-measured phone/SM variable → NOT_MET'.

FT_IC3.evidence: Criterion contingent on FT_IC2 MET; FT_IC2 fails

FT_IC4.evidence: Criterion contingent on FT_IC2 MET; FT_IC2 fails

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical longitudinal analysis examining cyberbullying victimization at T1 predicting brain activation and mental health outcomes at T2

FT_IC6.evidence: Published journal article in Journal of Medical Internet Research, 2026

FT_EC7_reason: FT_IC2 NOT_MET; exclusion criterion does not apply
notes: Paper fails at FT_IC2 (cyberbullying victimization without separately-measured smartphone/SM variable). Does not meet exposure criterion.
